body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

Claude Code has a first-class Task tool (a.k.a. Agent tool) that spawns sub-agents for parallel exploration. Each subagent writes an `isSidechain` transcript to `~/.claude/projects/<slug>/<parent>/subagents/agent-<uuid>.jsonl`. The parent waits for the subagent's completion signal â€” a `task-notification` user-event in the parent transcript carrying the subagent's task-id and status â€” before continuing its turn.

irrlicht's claudecode adapter observes the full topology via file-based discovery, not in-process counting:

1. The fswatcher walks `~/.claude/projects/` (transcriptsDir(), agent.go:36) and picks up every subagent transcript as its own `SessionState`.
2. The detector emits a `KindParentLinked` lifecycle event when the child session's `ParentSessionID` is set (session_detector_activity.go:110). The orchestrator-side scenario `subagent-spawn` already verifies this with `parent_linked_min: 2`.
3. `CountOpenSubagents` (parser.go:553) deliberately returns 0 because the file-based path is the single source of truth â€” counting in-process Agent tool_use entries as well would double-count. The metrics layer derives `SubagentCount(parent)` from the live children that link to it.
4. Parent state: the parent's outstanding Agent tool_use prevents `IsAgentDone()` from firing, so the parent stays `working` until the tool_result for the Task arrives. `handleTaskNotification` (parser.go:192) records the subagent completion signals from the parent transcript.

Mapped to spec bullets:
- 'Parent ready â†’ working at spawn' â€” parent's Agent tool_use line drives the transition.
- 'Child session appears with ParentSessionID = parent.session_id' â€” fswatcher discovers the sidechain file and links via lifecycle KindParentLinked.
- 'SubagentCount(parent) = N while any child alive' â€” derived from live linked children.
- 'Parent stays working until child ends' â€” parent's pending Agent tool_use blocks IsAgentDone.
- 'Parent â†’ ready after last child ends' â€” task-notification arrives, parent emits end_turn, workingâ†’ready.

The AskUserQuestion-from-child variant works because the child has its own independent transcript and tailer state, so its `workingâ†’waitingâ†’workingâ†’ready` arc is computed exactly like a parent's. The orphan-on-death variant is covered by the sweep â€” if the child process dies without an end-of-turn line, it's reaped by the same mechanism used in `subagent-orphan-cleanup` (assessed separately).
caveats: claudecode's subagent transcripts live under `<parent>/subagents/agent-*.jsonl` â€” a path that the curator's fixture bundler treats specially (`<fixture>.subagents/`). The orchestrator-side `subagent-spawn` recipe already validates the end-to-end shape; the agent-level coverage_id maps to `foreground-subagent` directly.

The N=3 parallel variant works because the file-based path scales linearly â€” each sidechain transcript is an independent SessionState. No special-case parser logic is needed for the 1-vs-3 distinction.

sources.note: CountOpenSubagents (line 553) returns 0 by design â€” file-based sidechain transcripts are the single source of truth, avoiding double-counting against open Agent tool_use entries.
lifecycle.KindParentLinked emitted at line 110 when a child SessionState's ParentSessionID is bound â€” this is what the orchestrator-side verify asserts.
subagent-spawn recipe (coverage_id: foreground-subagent) â€” prompts 3 parallel Explore subagents and asserts parent_linked_min: 2, subagent_transcripts_min: 2.
